Label: APAF-FR-01
Issue: Does not specify the frequency of data acquisition
Correction: The APAF system shall acquire telemetry data of the ASPERA-3 Experiment and Mars Express Orbit/Attitude every 24 hours.

Label: APAF-FR-02
Issue: Does not specify the format of IDFS data sets
Correction: The APAF system shall process all ASPERA-3 science data into IDFS data sets in XML format.

Label: APAF-FR-03
Issue: Does not specify the types of engineering and ancillary information
Correction: The APAF system shall process the engineering and ancillary information, including temperature, pressure, and voltage data, necessary for calibration and science validation into IDFS data sets.

Label: APAF-FR-05
Issue: Does not specify the duration for data storage
Correction: The ASPERA-3 and MEX OA telemetry data shall be stored on a local SwRI archive for a minimum of 5 years.

Label: APAF-FR-07
Issue: Does not specify the update frequency for web-based displays
Correction: Web-based displays of the most current ASPERA-3 data shall be updated every 6 hours for public view.

Label: APAF-PR-01
Issue: Does not specify the encryption method for password protection
Correction: The APAF system web server shall be password protected using AES-256 encryption where appropriate to allow only pertinent ASPERA-3 team members access.

Label: APAF-CR-02
Issue: Does not specify the backup frequency for the production computer
Correction: There shall be a UNIX-based APAF system production computer with an archival system that performs daily backups.

Label: APAF-DR-01a
Issue: Does not specify the error handling procedure for data distribution
Correction: ASPERA-3 IDFS data that are electronically distributed shall be provided to the ASPERA-3 Co-I’s within 24 hours of acquiring ASPERA-3 telemetry as long as the transmission and processing are error-free. In case of errors, the system shall retry the transmission every hour until successful.

Label: APAF-DR-02c
Issue: Does not specify the validation process for data before submission
Correction: ASPERA-3 data shall be provided to NASA PDS no later than 6 months after acquisition, following a thorough validation process to ensure data accuracy.

Label: APAF-DS-II-1
Issue: Does not specify the format for system parameters
Correction: System Parameters shall be provided in JSON format.

Label: APAF-DS-OI-1
Issue: Does not specify the method of data distribution
Correction: Distribution of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA IDFS Data Sets shall be performed via secure FTP.

Label: APAF-DS-OI-3
Issue: Does not specify the retention period for local archives
Correction: Local Archive of ASPERA-3 and MEX OA Telemetry & IDFS Data shall be retained for a minimum of 5 years.
